trivia_bullets.text: Fling is a reprint in ELD (October 2019). Original printing dates to Stronghold (1998); subsequent reprints include Magic 2011 and Amonkhet before ELD.

"Giants believe that tossing redcaps at knights is the best way to deal with both." — No named speaker. The joke solves two problems with one throw and describes the card's mechanic — sacrifice a creature, deal its power as damage — in narrative terms.

Redcaps in ELD lore are Wilds creatures who conduct sneak attacks and raids, adapting the Anglo-Scottish border folklore creature: a malevolent goblin who soaks its cap in victims' blood and must keep killing to survive.
Illustrated by Lie Setiawan, whose comedic framing — screaming redcap mid-arc, shredded cloak, distant mountains below — captures the card's dual tone of horror and slapstick.

related_cards.name: Raging Redcap
related_cards.note: The creature being flung; shares the Redcap creature type and Wilds folklore origin

related_cards.name: Brash Taunter
related_cards.note: Creature that explicitly benefits from Fling effects; popular combo partner in Commander

related_cards.name: Goblin Grenade
related_cards.note: Classic red 'sacrifice a goblin, deal damage' parallel; same design lineage
